--- title: One-Way ANOVA subtitle: Sample Problems # author: Guenther, Benjamin A. project: type: website output-dir: docs format: html: html-math-method: mathjax self-contained-math: false embed-resources: false jupyter: python3 --- # About the one-way ANOVA problems use the `RamdomData` class which requires: * an integer value for the number of groups * the sample size per group - all groups will have the same sample size * a call to the `anova()` method with `test = "one-way"` Each of the problems below sets factor levels using a random integer between 2 and 5. Per-group sample size is randomly set between 5 and 15. An example funciton call is included below ::: {#785d7483 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} n_groups = random.randint(2,5) sample_size = random.randint(5,15) RandomData(groups = n_groups, n = sample_size).anova(test = "one-way") ``` ::: # Problem 1 Given the following between-subjects data, use a one-way ANOVA with $\alpha = {0.01}$

```{=html}
A B C D
40 30 41 37
33 30 45 33
26 39 38 40
32 18 39 41
36 30 32 36
33 31 60 41
31 45 39 33
33 28 40 42
29 42 37 30
```
Summary statistics for these data:
$G = {1290} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {47998} \quad k = {4} \quad N = {36}$
$T_{A} = {293} \quad SS_{A} = {126.22}$
$T_{B} = {293} \quad SS_{B} = {540.22}$
$T_{C} = {371} \quad SS_{C} = {491.56}$
$T_{D} = {333} \quad SS_{D} = {148.0}$

State the Hypotheses
$H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D$
$H_1:$ At least one mean is different

The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {4.46}, \alpha = {0.01}$

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom
$$df_{total} = N - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {36} - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {35}$$

$$df_{between} = k - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {4} - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {3}$$

$$df_{within} = N - K$$
$$df_{within} = {36} - {4}$$
$$df_{within} = {32}$$

Calculate the Sum of Squares
$$SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}$$
$$SS_{total} = {47998} - \frac{1290^2}{36}$$
$$SS_{total} = {47998} - \frac{1664100}{36}$$
$$SS_{total} = {47998} - {46225.0}$$
$$SS_{total} = {1773.0}$$

$$SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}$$
$$SS_{within} = {126.22 + 540.22 + 491.56 + 148.0}$$
$$SS_{within} = {1306.0}$$

$$SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}$$
$$SS_{between} = {1773.0} - {1306.0}$$
$$SS_{between} = {467.0}$$

note: the other way to calculate $SS_{betwen}$ is:
$$SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$$

Calculate the Mean Squares
$$MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}$$
$$MS_{between} = \frac{467.0}{3}$$
$$MS_{between} = {155.67}$$

$$MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}$$
$$MS_{within} = \frac{1306.0}{32}$$
$$MS_{within} = {40.81}$$

Calculate the *F*-Ratio
$$F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}$$
$$F_{obt} = \frac{155.67}{40.81}$$
$$F_{obt} = {3.81}$$

Calculate $\eta^2$
$$\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}$$
$$\eta^2 = \frac{467.0}{1773.0}$$
$$\eta^2 = {0.26}$$

The results:

fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,

$F({3}, {32}) = {3.81}, p > {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.26}$ # Problem 2 Given the following between-subjects data, use a one-way ANOVA with $\alpha = {0.01}$

```{=html}
A B C
20 24 14
23 17 22
17 21 19
22 17 19
20 17 21
24 23 16
22 20 16
25 22 14
26 19 18
25 18 21
20 22 14
25 21 20
```
Summary statistics for these data:
$G = {724} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {14932} \quad k = {3} \quad N = {36}$
$T_{A} = {269} \quad SS_{A} = {82.92}$
$T_{B} = {241} \quad SS_{B} = {66.92}$
$T_{C} = {214} \quad SS_{C} = {95.67}$

State the Hypotheses
$H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C$
$H_1:$ At least one mean is different

The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {5.31}, \alpha = {0.01}$

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom
$$df_{total} = N - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {36} - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {35}$$

$$df_{between} = k - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {3} - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {2}$$

$$df_{within} = N - K$$
$$df_{within} = {36} - {3}$$
$$df_{within} = {33}$$

Calculate the Sum of Squares
$$SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}$$
$$SS_{total} = {14932} - \frac{724^2}{36}$$
$$SS_{total} = {14932} - \frac{524176}{36}$$
$$SS_{total} = {14932} - {14560.44}$$
$$SS_{total} = {371.56}$$

$$SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}$$
$$SS_{within} = {82.92 + 66.92 + 95.67}$$
$$SS_{within} = {245.51}$$

$$SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}$$
$$SS_{between} = {371.56} - {245.51}$$
$$SS_{between} = {126.05}$$

note: the other way to calculate $SS_{betwen}$ is:
$$SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$$

Calculate the Mean Squares
$$MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}$$
$$MS_{between} = \frac{126.05}{2}$$
$$MS_{between} = {63.02}$$

$$MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}$$
$$MS_{within} = \frac{245.51}{33}$$
$$MS_{within} = {7.44}$$

Calculate the *F*-Ratio
$$F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}$$
$$F_{obt} = \frac{63.02}{7.44}$$
$$F_{obt} = {8.47}$$

Calculate $\eta^2$
$$\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}$$
$$\eta^2 = \frac{126.05}{371.56}$$
$$\eta^2 = {0.34}$$

The results:

reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,

$F({2}, {33}) = {8.47}, p < {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.34}$ # Problem 3 Given the following between-subjects data, use a one-way ANOVA with $\alpha = {0.01}$

```{=html}
A B C D E
35 41 41 32 41
27 36 36 38 36
32 31 30 39 31
36 38 40 26 44
21 28 38 35 34
28 36 32 29 32
29 47 46 26 25
```
Summary statistics for these data:
$G = {1196} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {42158} \quad k = {5} \quad N = {35}$
$T_{A} = {208} \quad SS_{A} = {159.43}$
$T_{B} = {257} \quad SS_{B} = {235.43}$
$T_{C} = {263} \quad SS_{C} = {179.71}$
$T_{D} = {225} \quad SS_{D} = {174.86}$
$T_{E} = {243} \quad SS_{E} = {243.43}$

State the Hypotheses
$H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E$
$H_1:$ At least one mean is different

The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {4.02}, \alpha = {0.01}$

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom
$$df_{total} = N - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {35} - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {34}$$

$$df_{between} = k - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {5} - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {4}$$

$$df_{within} = N - K$$
$$df_{within} = {35} - {5}$$
$$df_{within} = {30}$$

Calculate the Sum of Squares
$$SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}$$
$$SS_{total} = {42158} - \frac{1196^2}{35}$$
$$SS_{total} = {42158} - \frac{1430416}{35}$$
$$SS_{total} = {42158} - {40869.03}$$
$$SS_{total} = {1288.97}$$

$$SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}$$
$$SS_{within} = {159.43 + 235.43 + 179.71 + 174.86 + 243.43}$$
$$SS_{within} = {992.86}$$

$$SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}$$
$$SS_{between} = {1288.97} - {992.86}$$
$$SS_{between} = {296.11}$$

note: the other way to calculate $SS_{betwen}$ is:
$$SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$$

Calculate the Mean Squares
$$MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}$$
$$MS_{between} = \frac{296.11}{4}$$
$$MS_{between} = {74.03}$$

$$MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}$$
$$MS_{within} = \frac{992.86}{30}$$
$$MS_{within} = {33.1}$$

Calculate the *F*-Ratio
$$F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}$$
$$F_{obt} = \frac{74.03}{33.1}$$
$$F_{obt} = {2.24}$$

Calculate $\eta^2$
$$\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}$$
$$\eta^2 = \frac{296.11}{1288.97}$$
$$\eta^2 = {0.23}$$

The results:

fail to reject the null hypothesis, results not significant,

$F({4}, {30}) = {2.24}, p > {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.23}$ # Problem 4 Given the following between-subjects data, use a one-way ANOVA with $\alpha = {0.05}$

```{=html}
A B C D E
63 46 59 80 49
55 81 66 69 43
51 91 36 77 41
55 71 69 71 40
48 76 59 78 64
49 84 49 64 53
51 90 61 78 41
48 63 72 57 61
57 91 63 54 58
31 82 77 57 38
44 67 79 60 68
39 90 56 81 38
65 86 46 63 42
84 68 38 67 61
```
Summary statistics for these data:
$G = {4309} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {281835} \quad k = {5} \quad N = {70}$
$T_{A} = {740} \quad SS_{A} = {2063.71}$
$T_{B} = {1086} \quad SS_{B} = {2271.43}$
$T_{C} = {830} \quad SS_{C} = {2308.86}$
$T_{D} = {956} \quad SS_{D} = {1146.86}$
$T_{E} = {697} \quad SS_{E} = {1498.36}$

State the Hypotheses
$H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E$
$H_1:$ At least one mean is different

The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {2.51}, \alpha = {0.05}$

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom
$$df_{total} = N - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {70} - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {69}$$

$$df_{between} = k - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {5} - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {4}$$

$$df_{within} = N - K$$
$$df_{within} = {70} - {5}$$
$$df_{within} = {65}$$

Calculate the Sum of Squares
$$SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}$$
$$SS_{total} = {281835} - \frac{4309^2}{70}$$
$$SS_{total} = {281835} - \frac{18567481}{70}$$
$$SS_{total} = {281835} - {265249.73}$$
$$SS_{total} = {16585.27}$$

$$SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}$$
$$SS_{within} = {2063.71 + 2271.43 + 2308.86 + 1146.86 + 1498.36}$$
$$SS_{within} = {9289.22}$$

$$SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}$$
$$SS_{between} = {16585.27} - {9289.22}$$
$$SS_{between} = {7296.05}$$

note: the other way to calculate $SS_{betwen}$ is:
$$SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$$

Calculate the Mean Squares
$$MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}$$
$$MS_{between} = \frac{7296.05}{4}$$
$$MS_{between} = {1824.01}$$

$$MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}$$
$$MS_{within} = \frac{9289.22}{65}$$
$$MS_{within} = {142.91}$$

Calculate the *F*-Ratio
$$F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}$$
$$F_{obt} = \frac{1824.01}{142.91}$$
$$F_{obt} = {12.76}$$

Calculate $\eta^2$
$$\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}$$
$$\eta^2 = \frac{7296.05}{16585.27}$$
$$\eta^2 = {0.44}$$

The results:

reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,

$F({4}, {65}) = {12.76}, p < {0.05}, \eta^2 = {0.44}$ # Problem 5 Given the following between-subjects data, use a one-way ANOVA with $\alpha = {0.01}$

```{=html}
A B C D E
47 51 41 31 37
38 54 37 30 42
30 44 41 23 31
42 50 37 34 21
43 43 34 36 36
43 38 45 42 47
34 40 48 28 38
33 38 31 31 40
32 38 39 24 40
```
Summary statistics for these data:
$G = {1702} \quad \Sigma{X^2} = {66696} \quad k = {5} \quad N = {45}$
$T_{A} = {342} \quad SS_{A} = {288.0}$
$T_{B} = {396} \quad SS_{B} = {310.0}$
$T_{C} = {353} \quad SS_{C} = {221.56}$
$T_{D} = {279} \quad SS_{D} = {278.0}$
$T_{E} = {332} \quad SS_{E} = {436.89}$

State the Hypotheses
$H_0: \mu_A = \mu_B = \mu_C = \mu_D = \mu_E$
$H_1:$ At least one mean is different

The decision criteria:

$F_{crit} = {3.83}, \alpha = {0.01}$

Calculate the Degrees of Freedom
$$df_{total} = N - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {45} - 1$$
$$df_{total} = {44}$$

$$df_{between} = k - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {5} - 1$$
$$df_{between} = {4}$$

$$df_{within} = N - K$$
$$df_{within} = {45} - {5}$$
$$df_{within} = {40}$$

Calculate the Sum of Squares
$$SS_{total} = \Sigma X^2 - \frac{G^2}{N}$$
$$SS_{total} = {66696} - \frac{1702^2}{45}$$
$$SS_{total} = {66696} - \frac{2896804}{45}$$
$$SS_{total} = {66696} - {64373.42}$$
$$SS_{total} = {2322.58}$$

$$SS_{within} = \Sigma SS_{inside\_each\_condition}$$
$$SS_{within} = {288.0 + 310.0 + 221.56 + 278.0 + 436.89}$$
$$SS_{within} = {1534.45}$$

$$SS_{between} = SS_{total} - SS_{within}$$
$$SS_{between} = {2322.58} - {1534.45}$$
$$SS_{between} = {788.13}$$

note: the other way to calculate $SS_{betwen}$ is:
$$SS_{between} = \Sigma{\frac{T^2}{n}} - \frac{G^2}{N}$$

Calculate the Mean Squares
$$MS_{between} = \frac{SS_{between}}{df_{between}}$$
$$MS_{between} = \frac{788.13}{4}$$
$$MS_{between} = {197.03}$$

$$MS_{within} = \frac{SS_{within}}{df_{within}}$$
$$MS_{within} = \frac{1534.45}{40}$$
$$MS_{within} = {38.36}$$

Calculate the *F*-Ratio
$$F_{obt} = \frac{MS_{between}}{MS_{within}}$$
$$F_{obt} = \frac{197.03}{38.36}$$
$$F_{obt} = {5.14}$$

Calculate $\eta^2$
$$\eta^2 = \frac{SS{between}}{SS_{total}}$$
$$\eta^2 = \frac{788.13}{2322.58}$$
$$\eta^2 = {0.34}$$

The results:

reject the null hypothesis, results are significant,

$F({4}, {40}) = {5.14}, p < {0.01}, \eta^2 = {0.34}$
